title: Venture Deals
subtitle: Be Smarter Than Your Lawyer and Venture Capitalist
author: Brad Feld, Jason Mendelson
excerpt: The definitive guide to understanding venture capital term sheets, deal structures, and the entrepreneur-VC relationship — written by a seasoned VC and a startup lawyer.
keyIdeas: The term sheet is the DNA of the VC deal
Liquidation preference determines who gets paid first
Anti-dilution provisions protect investors from down rounds
Board control is more important than valuation
The entrepreneur-VC relationship is a long-term partnership
keyTakeaways: Valuation is not the most important term — control and economics matter more
Liquidation preference multiplies determine real investor returns
Pay-to-play provisions can force entrepreneurs into bad deals
A good VC relationship is worth more than a high valuation
whoShouldSkip: Anyone not dealing with VC term sheets
